description: Real-time morse code decoder web app with microphone/file input, spectrogram visualization, adaptive CW decoding, text-to-morse encoding, and session persistence for authenticated users

userStories.title: Rename package scope from @acme to @moris-bot
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the monorepo packages renamed from @acme to @moris-bot so the project has its own identity before building features.
acceptanceCriteria: All @acme/ references replaced with @moris-bot/ across all files (package.json, tsconfig, eslint configs, source imports, CSS imports)
turbo/generators/config.ts updated: .startsWith('@acme/') and .replace('@acme/', '') changed to @moris-bot, 'Acme Monorepo' text updated
Root package.json name updated from create-t3-turbo to moris-bot
App metadata (layout titles, descriptions) updated from 'Create T3 Turbo' to 'Moris Bot'
pnpm-lock.yaml deleted and regenerated via pnpm install
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a mechanical global find-and-replace. Must be done first so all subsequent work uses the new scope.

userStories.title: Create morse-decoder package scaffold with types, constants, and morse tree
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the foundational morse-decoder package with shared types, configuration constants, and the ITU morse code lookup tree.
acceptanceCriteria: New package at packages/morse-decoder with name @moris-bot/morse-decoder
package.json follows existing conventions (exports, scripts, devDependencies on @moris-bot/eslint-config, @moris-bot/prettier-config, @moris-bot/tsconfig)
tsconfig.json extends @moris-bot/tsconfig/base.json
eslint.config.ts uses @moris-bot/eslint-config/base
src/types.ts exports: DecoderConfig, DecoderEvents, DecoderStats, MorseElement ('dit' | 'dah'), MorseGap ('element' | 'character' | 'word'), GoertzelResult interfaces
src/constants.ts exports default values: targetFrequency (600), sampleRate (8000), blockSize (256), initialWpm (15), timing ratios (dit=1, dah=3, charGap=3, wordGap=7)
src/morse-tree.ts exports lookupMorse(elements: MorseElement[]): string | null and getMorseForChar(char: string): MorseElement[] | null covering A-Z, 0-9, and standard punctuation
src/index.ts re-exports all public API
Package has zero runtime dependencies
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation package. The morse tree is a binary tree: left=dit, right=dah. Must cover full ITU international morse code.

userStories.title: Implement Goertzel filter and envelope detector
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Goertzel single-frequency detector and envelope detector so audio samples can be analyzed for morse tone presence.
acceptanceCriteria: src/goertzel.ts exports createGoertzelFilter(config: GoertzelConfig) returning { process(samples: Float32Array): GoertzelResult }
Goertzel filter correctly detects magnitude at target frequency using the standard second-order IIR algorithm
GoertzelResult includes magnitude (linear) and magnitudeDb (dBFS)
src/envelope-detector.ts exports createEnvelopeDetector(config: EnvelopeConfig) returning { process(magnitude: number, timestampMs: number): EnvelopeState }
Envelope detector uses dual-threshold hysteresis (separate on/off thresholds) to avoid jitter
EnvelopeState includes toneActive (boolean), durationMs (time in current state), snrDb
Both modules exported from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Goertzel is ~30 lines of arithmetic. Envelope detector compares magnitude against thresholds and tracks state transitions with timestamps.

userStories.title: Implement timing analyzer and decoder state machine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the timing analyzer and main decoder orchestrator so the full pipeline from audio samples to decoded text is functional.
acceptanceCriteria: src/timing-analyzer.ts exports createTimingAnalyzer(config: TimingConfig) with onToneEnd(durationMs) returning MorseElement and onSilenceEnd(durationMs) returning MorseGap
Timing analyzer classifies durations using configurable WPM-derived unit timing
Adaptive mode uses exponential moving average on observed dit durations to track sender speed
getCurrentWpm() returns the current estimated WPM
src/decoder.ts exports createDecoder(config: DecoderConfig, events: DecoderEvents) returning MorseDecoder
MorseDecoder has methods: processSamples(samples: Float32Array, timestampMs: number), getDecodedText(), reset(), updateConfig(partial)
processSamples orchestrates: Goertzel -> envelope detector -> timing analyzer -> morse tree lookup
Events fire correctly: onCharacter, onWordBoundary, onElement, onToneChange, onStats
Both modules exported from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This completes the decode pipeline. The decoder state machine tracks accumulated elements and flushes characters on character/word gaps.

userStories.title: Implement morse encoder module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a morse encoding module so text can be converted to morse timing data for audio playback.
acceptanceCriteria: src/encoder.ts exports createEncoder(config: { wpm: number; frequency: number }) returning MorseEncoder
MorseEncoder has encode(text: string) method returning array of { type: 'tone' | 'silence'; durationMs: number }
Uses getMorseForChar from morse-tree.ts for character lookup
Timing derived from WPM: dit duration = 1200/wpm ms, dah = 3x dit, element gap = 1x dit, character gap = 3x dit, word gap = 7x dit
Unknown characters are skipped without error
Exported from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create AudioWorklet sample forwarder and useAudioInput hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Web Audio API microphone capture with an AudioWorklet so audio samples can be forwarded to the decoder on the main thread.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/nextjs/public/audio-worklet/sample-forwarder.js created as a minimal AudioWorklet processor
Worklet process() method posts Float32Array input samples via this.port.postMessage()
apps/nextjs/src/hooks/use-audio-input.ts exports useAudioInput() hook
Hook provides: isRecording (boolean), error (string | null), startRecording(), stopRecording()
startRecording() calls getUserMedia({ audio: true }), creates AudioContext, registers worklet, connects nodes
stopRecording() disconnects nodes, closes AudioContext, stops media tracks
Hook accepts an onSamples callback ref that receives Float32Array chunks from the worklet
Graceful error handling for permission denial and unsupported browsers
@moris-bot/morse-decoder added to apps/nextjs/package.json dependencies and next.config.js transpilePackages
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The worklet must be a plain .js file in public/ because AudioWorklet requires a URL, not a bundled module. Sample rate should be 8000 Hz for efficiency.

userStories.title: Create useMorseDecoder hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a React hook that wraps the @moris-bot/morse-decoder package with React state management.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/nextjs/src/hooks/use-morse-decoder.ts exports useMorseDecoder(config: DecoderConfig) hook
Hook creates and manages a MorseDecoder instance via createDecoder()
Exposes processSamples(samples: Float32Array, timestampMs: number) for the audio hook to call
Maintains React state for: decodedText (string), stats (DecoderStats), currentElements (MorseElement[])
State updates throttled via requestAnimationFrame to avoid excessive re-renders
Exposes reset() to clear decoded text and reset decoder
Exposes updateConfig(partial) to update decoder settings
Decoder instance recreated when config changes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove demo content and create decoder page shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want the decoder to be the home page instead of the demo posts content.
acceptanceCriteria: Demo posts CRUD UI removed from apps/nextjs/src/app/page.tsx
Demo auth showcase component removed from home page
apps/nextjs/src/app/page.tsx renders a new DecoderPanel client component
apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/decoder-panel.tsx created as 'use client' component
DecoderPanel composes useAudioInput and useMorseDecoder hooks, wiring onSamples to processSamples
DecoderPanel renders a basic layout: start/stop button at top, decoded text area at bottom
Existing infrastructure (auth, db, tRPC) remains functional and accessible
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This creates the minimal working decoder page. Subsequent stories add spectrogram, stats, and controls.

userStories.title: Add decoded text output component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see decoded morse code as readable text with auto-scrolling and in-progress element display.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/decoded-text.tsx created
Scrollable text area displays decoded characters in monospace font
Text area auto-scrolls to bottom as new characters arrive
Shows in-progress element accumulation (dots and dashes for current unfinished character) at the end
Word boundaries rendered as spaces
Component integrated into DecoderPanel
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add signal statistics display component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see signal statistics (dBFS, frequency, SNR, WPM) so I can monitor decoding quality.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/signal-stats.tsx created
Displays current signal level in dBFS
Displays target frequency in Hz
Displays signal-to-noise ratio in dB
Displays current detected/estimated WPM
Receives DecoderStats as props from DecoderPanel
Uses @moris-bot/ui components for styling (labels, separators)
Component integrated into DecoderPanel layout
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add decoder controls component
userStories.description: As a user, I want controls to adjust decoder settings like frequency, speed, and threshold.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/decoder-controls.tsx created
Target frequency slider/input with range 300-1200 Hz (default 600)
WPM speed preset selector with options: 5, 10, 13, 15, 20, 25, 30
Adaptive mode toggle (on by default)
Detection threshold adjustment slider
Clear/reset button to clear decoded text and reset decoder state
Controls built using @moris-bot/ui components (Button, Input, Label)
Control changes call updateConfig on the decoder via DecoderPanel
Component integrated into DecoderPanel layout (top controls bar)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add spectrogram/waterfall canvas display
userStories.description: As a user, I want a visual spectrogram showing signal magnitude over time so I can see morse code patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/nextjs/src/app/_components/spectrogram.tsx created
HTML canvas element with 2D rendering context
Scrolling waterfall: new data drawn as column on right, existing data scrolls left
Signal intensity represented by color gradient (dark = silence, bright/green = tone)
Renders via requestAnimationFrame, independent of React render cycle
Receives magnitude data from DecoderStats via a callback or ref pattern
Canvas sized responsively to container width
Component integrated into DecoderPanel layout (middle section)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use a ring buffer or ImageData shift pattern for efficient scrolling. Canvas rendering must not cause React re-renders.

userStories.title: Add audio file upload and decoding
userStories.description: As a user, I want to upload audio files containing morse code to decode recordings.
acceptanceCriteria: File upload input added to decoder controls accepting .wav, .mp3, .ogg, .m4a formats
Uploaded file decoded via Web Audio API decodeAudioData
Decoded samples fed into @moris-bot/morse-decoder processSamples() in chunks (matching blockSize)
Processing runs asynchronously with progress indicator
Decoded text displayed in the same decoded-text component as live decoding
User can stop/cancel file processing mid-stream
Cannot use mic and file input simultaneously (file stops mic, mic stops file)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use setTimeout or requestAnimationFrame to chunk processing so the UI remains responsive during file decode.

userStories.title: Add text-to-morse encoder with audio playback
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type text and hear it played as morse code audio.
acceptanceCriteria: Encoder section added below decoder (accessible via tab or toggle)
Text input area for entering text to encode
Play button generates morse code audio using Web Audio API OscillatorNode
Uses createEncoder() from @moris-bot/morse-decoder to produce timing events
Playback frequency matches decoder target frequency setting
Playback WPM configurable (uses decoder WPM setting by default)
Stop button halts playback mid-stream
Visual indication of current character being played (highlight in input text)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Schedule OscillatorNode start/stop times using AudioContext.currentTime for precise timing. Do not use setTimeout for tone timing.

userStories.title: Add decoded sessions database schema and tRPC router
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a database table and API endpoints for saving decoded sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: New decoded_sessions table in packages/db/src/schema.ts with columns: id, userId, decodedText, durationMs, source ('mic' | 'file'), settings (JSON), createdAt
userId references the auth user table
Zod schema generated via drizzle-zod for the new table
New tRPC router in packages/api/src/router/session.ts with protected procedures: save, list, getById, delete
Session router added to the app router in packages/api/src/root.ts
List procedure returns sessions for the authenticated user only, ordered by createdAt desc
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use protectedProcedure from the existing tRPC setup to ensure auth. Settings column stores decoder config as JSON.

userStories.title: Add save session button to decoder (authenticated users)
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to save my decoded session so I can review it later.
acceptanceCriteria: Save Session button visible in decoder controls area only when user is authenticated
Unauthenticated users see a subtle 'Sign in to save sessions' link instead
Clicking Save Session calls the session.save tRPC mutation with current decoded text, duration, source, and settings
Success toast shown after saving via @moris-bot/ui toast component
Error toast shown if save fails
Button disabled when there is no decoded text to save
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add sessions list page with view and export
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view my saved sessions and export them as text files.
acceptanceCriteria: New page at apps/nextjs/src/app/sessions/page.tsx
Sessions list shows saved sessions with timestamp, duration, source type, and preview text (first 100 chars)
Click a session to expand and view full decoded text
Export button downloads the session's decoded text as a .txt file
Delete button removes a session with confirmation dialog
Only shows sessions belonging to the authenticated user
Redirects to home if not authenticated
Navigation link to /sessions visible when authenticated
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Responsive layout and theme polish
userStories.description: As a user, I want the decoder page to be well-organized and usable across screen sizes with proper theme support.
acceptanceCriteria: Decoder layout: controls bar (top), spectrogram + stats side-by-side (middle), text output (bottom), encoder toggle (below decoder)
Responsive breakpoints: desktop (1024px+) shows full layout, tablet (768px+) stacks spectrogram above stats
Dark/light theme works correctly via existing ThemeProvider and ThemeToggle
Dark theme uses signal-processing aesthetic (dark background, bright signal colors, monospace text)
All components visually consistent and properly spaced
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Final polish story. Mobile web should be functional but the Expo app is the intended mobile experience.
